title: Smithiantha cinnabarina
taxonomy: Smithiantha cinnabarina is classified within the family Gesneriaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This family is further grouped into the order Lamiales, a diverse group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is part of the larger clade of asterids, which also includes the orders Apiales and Dipsacales. The clade of asterids is a subgroup of the larger clade of angiosperms, which contains all flowering plants. intro: Smithiantha cinnabarina, also known as Brazilian pink nightshade, is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family, Solanaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Smithiantha cinnabarina include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of tinctures and extracts, which are often used to treat conditions such as hypertension, fever, and digestive issues.
